name: Error-boundary UX recipe
description: Wraps any component that renders fetched data; renders a recoverable error UI if rendering throws.
shipped: This base SHIPS the boundary at frontend/src/lib/ErrorBoundary.jsx. Import and use it - do NOT hand-build one. The shell already mounts it at the root and around each page; add more around risky subtrees.
usage:
import { ErrorBoundary } from './lib/ErrorBoundary';
<ErrorBoundary fallback={({ error, retry }) => (...)} onError={(e) => log(e)}>
  <DataView />
</ErrorBoundary>

props.fallback: ({ error, retry }) => ReactNode (optional; replaces the default card)

visualRules: Card with border var(--color-border), radius var(--radius-md), padding var(--space-6)
Primary action: "Tentar de novo" - calls retry()

rules: Every page-level data render must sit inside this boundary.
React boundaries only catch render errors: catch an async fetch in the component, put the error in state, and render it inside the boundary - never swallow it silently.
Never let an unhandled exception escape to React's default error UI.
